headline: How 80,000 Voters Outweigh 180,000
context: Malaysia's electoral boundaries give Putrajaya (20,834 voters) and Kapar (179,000 voters) each one parliamentary seat — a 9:1 disparity in vote weight.

cards.big: One vote in Putrajaya equals nine votes in Kapar.
cards.sub: Same Parliament. Same sovereignty. Radically different representation.

cards.big: Rural seats average 40,000 voters. Urban seats average 110,000.
cards.sub: Article 46 sets no maximum variance. The EC has used this silence aggressively.

cards.big: 2018 redelineation gave BN 54% of seats with 48% of votes.
cards.sub: Malapportionment is not a side effect. It is the electoral architecture.

cards.big: The EC reports to the PM's Department, not Parliament.
cards.sub: The body that draws boundaries answers to the party that benefits from them.

cards.big: You are not voting in the same election as your neighbour.
cards.sub: The weight of your vote was decided before you cast it.

cards.big: Democracy assumes equal votes. Malaysia's boundaries guarantee unequal ones. The map is the policy.
